Luxury catalog product photography in the register of Holderness & Bourne, photorealistic, sharp focus edge to edge across the entire flat garment, true-to-life fabric and knit-yarn detail, high-end resolution, evenly lit and fully in focus as a top-down flat-lay. Studio product photography, soft diffused overhead light with a gentle even fill, delicate soft natural shadow beneath the fabric edges, a soft low matte sheen on the pique knit. Seamless warm-cream paper background (#F4EFE4), evenly lit. Premium menswear catalog photography in the refined heritage-prep register of Holderness & Bourne, photorealistic, ultra sharp focus edge to edge across the whole flat garment, true-to-life fabric weave and stitch detail, high-end resolution, even uniform focus with no blur.

Soft, even, diffused studio light from the upper left, the garment separated from the background by a softly graduated lighter halo in the background paper (not a hard specular line on the fabric), gentle natural contact shadow beneath the edges. Luxury catalog product photography in the understated register of Holderness & Bourne, photorealistic, true-to-life brushed-fleece detail, sharp overall focus with only a whisper of softness at the outer edges, high-end resolution. Render at 1:1 aspect ratio.

prompt: Standalone flat illustration of a single 1930s rubber-hose cartoon golfer in the Cuphead / Fleischer-Studios style, rendered as a clean centered digital graphic on a solid even warm-cream field (warm cream, hex F4EFE4) with generous empty margins on all four sides and no border, no vignette, no gradient, the background a single flat tone so the figure knocks out cleanly for compositing. One cheerful anthropomorphic golfer only, no second character and no mascot, drawn with bold confident black ink outlines of varying weight, classic rubber-hose noodle arms and legs, rounded soft-bodied proportions, big four-fingered white gloves, pie-cut cartoon eyes and a wide friendly grin, wearing a flat newsboy cap and plus-four knickers, following through mid golf swing with a slim hickory club, a small clean motion-arc line and one tiny golf ball on a tee. Vintage hand-inked-and-painted animation-cel look with gentle cross-hatch shading, soft film grain and a faint print-registration texture baked flat into the artwork itself. Strictly limited on-brand palette of forest felt green (deep forest green, hex 2F4A3A), brass gold (warm antique gold, hex B4893E) and claret red (muted oxblood red, hex 8E3A3A) accents over ink-black line and cream, with white gloves; no other hues, no skin tones, no blues. Every surface is wordless and lettering-free: cap, ball, club, clothing and background carry no text, no numbers, no banners, no pennants, no signage, no title cards and no readable characters of any kind. Crisp vector-clean line art, perfectly even flat reproduction lighting, no photographic depth of field, no glare, no paper curl, no drop shadow under the figure, the complete figure sitting fully inside the frame with clear breathing room. High-resolution vintage cartoon illustration as finished flat print art, not a photograph of an object.
colorway: Ink-black line + cream ground with forest green (#2F4A3A), brass (#B4893E), claret (#8E3A3A) accents — palette-locked, no off-brand hues; this is the standalone composite source for the leisure tee + swing-anim reference

Luxury heritage-golf catalog product photography in the Holderness & Bourne register, photorealistic, sharp focus on the front row, shallow depth of field softening the rear cover, ultra-high-resolution, fine detail. Square 1:1 framing, eye-level three-quarter product angle.
